description_long: This object consists of a cluster of mid-rise apartment buildings arranged in a loose rectangular formation, each with distinct facades featuring brick, concrete, and painted surfaces. The buildings vary in height and width, with some displaying faded red or brown brickwork, while others have green or beige painted exteriors. Windows are regularly spaced in vertical and horizontal rows, with some facades showing more ornate window framing. The rooftops are flat and appear to be made of concrete or tar, typical of urban apartment complexes. The overall appearance suggests older, utilitarian residential structures commonly found in dense city neighborhoods. There are no visible balconies, and entrances are not clearly defined from these views.
description: A group of mid-rise, brick and concrete apartment buildings with flat roofs and regularly spaced windows, arranged in a compact urban block.
description_view_0: From this angle, the green-painted rear wall of one building is prominent, with several brick and beige facades visible in the background.
description_view_1: This view highlights the front-facing facades with more visible windows and a variety of brick and painted surfaces, showing the buildings' depth and arrangement.
description_view_2: The arrangement of the buildings is clearer, with the tallest brick building in the foreground and a mix of windowed facades and painted surfaces.
description_view_3: The tallest building's side is most visible here, along with a mix of brick and green-painted exteriors, emphasizing the block-like structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, evenly spaced vertical and horizontal rows, creating a grid-like pattern across the facades.
